# Transportation Impact Brief
## Maria — Concerned Elementary Parent (PERSONA-001)

---

## Executive Summary

If Option A moves forward, 139–169 elementary families — 18–24% of all elementary households — will split children across two buildings with different bell times. After-care is already full at four of five elementary schools. The families who can't absorb that gap face $144K–$804K in collective annual child care costs that no district budget line covers. The district decided to cut 14% of its bus drivers before anyone modeled whether the remaining drivers could run the routes.

---

## Key Finding

**Up to $803,520 per year in new child care costs would fall on families under Option A — with zero new district support proposed.**

That number comes from 42–144 families who currently rely on school-based before/after care and would lose that access due to mismatched bell schedules under a 4-tier routing system. At market rates for licensed child care ($57/day × 180 school days), the per-family exposure is $10,260/year. Option B produces $0 in family care costs.

---

## Configuration Comparison

| | **Option A** | **Option B** | **Variant C** |
|---|---|---|---|
| Split families | 139–169 (18–24%) | **0** | 123–150 (16–21%) |
| Bus tiers needed | 3–4 | 3 | 3–4 |
| Drivers needed | 30 | 24 | 29 |
| Drivers available (post-cut) | 17–20 | 17–20 | 17–20 |
| Driver shortfall | **–13 to –10** | –7 to –4 | –12 to –9 |
| Care gap families | 42–144 | **0** | 37–128 |
| Annual family care cost | $144K–$804K | **$0** | $127K–$714K |
| Total fiscal exposure | $946K–$1.68M | **$30K–$80K** | $797K–$1.45M |
| As % of claimed savings | 43–112% | 1.4–5.3% | 36–97% |

**Driver shortfall is universal.** Even Option B — the lowest-impact configuration — requires 4–7 more drivers than will be available after the SEA cut. This is not a margin-of-error problem. The cut was decided before route counts were modeled (confirmed at the 3/30/2026 board meeting by Director of Operations Mike Natalie).

---

## What's Missing

The district has not produced analysis on four questions parents have raised repeatedly since February 4:

1. **Care gap by school.** After-care is confirmed full at 4 of 5 elementary schools, with only the closing school (Kaler) having openings. Which specific schools would generate the highest care demand under Option A or Variant C? This mapping does not exist in any budget document.

2. **Split-family identity.** The 139–169 split-family count is a model estimate. The district has enrollment records that could identify *exactly* which families are affected by address and sibling pairs. That analysis has not been published.

3. **Driver recruitment timeline and cost.** The shortfall of 10–13 drivers under Option A is not a planning contingency — it is a structural gap. No timeline, wage projection, or recruitment budget has been presented.

4. **Bell schedule impact on split families.** A 4th routing tier creates a 30-minute gap between drop-off windows. The driver idle window (9:30 AM–1:30 PM, confirmed 3/30) leaves no room for a 4th tier without overtime. No overtime cost estimate has been published.

---

## Limitations

This analysis models fiscal exposure from published enrollment data and market-rate child care costs. It does not have access to:

- Actual family-level enrollment records (sibling pairs, addresses)
- District transportation vendor contract terms
- Current contracted child care rates at South Portland programs
- The transportation consultant's working assumptions or route models

The care gap estimate ($144K–$804K) assumes 100% of displaced families pay market rate. Real cost depends on how many families have other options — relatives, informal care, flexible work. The range is wide by design; the district has the data to narrow it.

---

## Invitation to Improve

The district can sharpen every number in this brief with data it already holds:

- **Enrollment database** — sibling pairs and addresses would convert the 139–169 split-family estimate into an exact count
- **Before/after care enrollment records** — would identify which families currently depend on school-based care and are at risk under each configuration
- **Transportation consultant deliverables** — route models, driver assumptions, and cost projections were promised at the March 2 meeting; they have not been published
- **SEA contract terms** — driver availability post-cut depends on which positions are eliminated and whether recall rights apply

This brief will be updated when that data is available. Parents asking these questions at public comment are asking for this analysis — not opinion.

---

*Sources: SPEC-060 through SPEC-065; Transportation Claims Catalog (TC-001 through TC-015); Director of Operations confirmation, 3/30/2026 board meeting.*
